target.name: Sakana AI
target.founder: David Ha, Llion Jones, Ren Ito

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $12.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Thinking Machines Lab
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Seed), post-money $12B

data.narrative:
Sakana AI is a seed-stage AI & Software company founded by David Ha, Llion Jones, and Ren Ito, currently valued at $1.0B+ post-money on $230M+ raised. The peer universe comprises 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comps (Thinking Machines Lab, Liquid AI, Field AI, Twelve Labs), supplemented by reference to 1 public comparable (Palantir) and 8 private peers including OpenAI, Anthropic, and xAI.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.5x multiplier to the $1.0B last post-money valuation, implying $2.5B. Peer-median analysis across 4 same-sector comps yields a median valuation of $2.2B, with a range spanning $250M to $12.0B. These methods converge closely, with stage-progression and peer-median differing by only $300M.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.8B (low) to $2.9B (high), with a median of $2.3B. This represents a 23–190% step-up from the current $1.0B valuation. The primary uncertainty is the extreme dispersion within the peer set—Thinking Machines Lab trades at $12.0B while Twelve Labs sits at $250M—reflecting inconsistent market pricing for seed and Series A AI software businesses. Sakana's position within this range will depend on product differentiation, capital efficiency, and milestone achievement relative to peers.
